source_jobs.title: Designer, Brand & Product
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a seasoned designer with experience from the startup world to own how Redpine looks, feels, and functions everywhere it shows up: website, product interfaces, user-facing apps, social, and customer communication. You'll partner closely with Product, Engineering, and GTM to define what world-class design means for a company building the licensed knowledge layer for AI.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the Redpine brand end to end: web, product UI, social, customer communication, and every partner-facing surface.

 - Design in code, not just in Figma. You use AI coding tools daily from idea to prototypes.

 - Ensure one coherent experience across touch points

 - Create design from early concept to shipped product, across marketing, web, and in-app experiences.

 - Define and evolve the visual system and brand guidelines so they scale cleanly as the product and team grow.

 - Work directly with engineers to get design into production.

 - Set the design process and craft bar for the company as the team grows around you.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - 3+ years of professional design experience across web, product UI, and brand or visual identity from fast moving companies, ideally in tech.

 - A portfolio that shows real UX thinking, strong visual craft, and product judgment.

 - Fluency in Figma or equivalent, plus the ability to prototype in code. You should be comfortable using AI coding tools (Claude, Cursor, Codex or similar) to build functional prototypes yourself.

 - An AI-native way of working. You treat AI as leverage on your craft.

 - The judgment to know when pixel-perfect matters and when shipped is better than perfect.

 - Experience in building or evolving a design system and a brand's visual language.

 - Clear communication and a genuine growth mindset.




BONUS

 - 3D and animation design for landing page hero pieces or interactive brand moments

 - Data visualization craft, distinct from general UI, since Redpine's story is inherently about data flowing through a licensed system

 - Front-end fluency beyond prototyping (React, Tailwind) so prototypes can sometimes ship as-is rather than getting rebuilt by engineering

 - Design ops or tooling experience (Figma libraries, tokens, component governance) if you want this person setting up the system, not just using it




WHY REDPINE

 - Build the design foundation for an API driven company creating trust in the field of data.

 - Work in a global-first, AI-native team alongside operators from Spotify, Sana, Zettle, Lunar, and Pleo.

 - Competitive base salary with meaningful equity upside.
   
   


ABOUT REDPINE

Redpine https://redpine.ai/ is an AI-native, global-first company building the knowledge layer for AI. We unlock compliant access to high-quality, non-public, multi-modal data in fields like medicine, science, law, and finance, enabling data owners to monetize their content in a controlled way.

We embrace a culture of curiosity, speed, innovation and excellence. We believe in continuous learning, experimentation, and pushing the boundaries of what's possible in AI technology. You'll be working closely with the founding team, including Anders (ex-partner at VC, McKinsey), and David (ex-tech and product leader at unicorn companies Spotify, Zettle and Lunar).

We prioritize an office-first culture (in Stockholm) to fuel collaboration and connection, but we’re big on autonomy - for those days you need a "deep work" day at home or have life admin to handle.

If you are eager to make a meaningful impact in the AI space, we’d love to hear from you. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive team.

if you're excited about this role but your experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway.

source_jobs.title: Applied AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We interview on an ongoing basis. Please submit your application sooner rather than later.



We are seeking an innovative Applied AI Engineer to own the definition, scaling and expansion of AI/ML solutions at Redpine. Our mission is to be the leading knowledge layer for trusted AI data. In this role, you will be instrumental in developing and implementing machine learning solutions that power our platform, enabling organizations to efficiently source, validate, and manage high-quality training and inference data for their AI models. You will be among the first key employees building sophisticated algorithms for data quality assessment, automated validation and evaluation systems, and intelligent data matching solutions.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design and implement machine learning models for data quality assessment and validation

 - Develop algorithms for automated data labeling and annotation verification

 - Create and maintain data processing pipelines for various AI training data types

 - Develop and optimize matching algorithms between data providers and buyers

 - Implement scalable solutions for processing and analyzing large datasets

 - Collaborate with the product team to develop new features for the marketplace platform

 - Research and implement state-of-the-art ML techniques for data quality improvement

 - Work with customers and stakeholders to understand and implement data requirements for various AI use cases


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Strong experience in developing, training and deploying AI / Machine Learning models

 - Proficiency in Python and ML frameworks (PyTorch, R, Scala, Huggingface)

 - Knowledge of model deployment techniques and serving frameworks like TensorFlow Serving, TorchServe, or custom APIs

 - Experience with large-scale data processing and distributed computing

 - Experience with containerization and orchestration tools like Docker and Kubernetes as well as version control tools such as Git.

 - Strong communication skills and ability to work in a fast-paced environment

 - Comfortable writing queries, exploring data, and collaborating on hypotheses with product and engineering counterpart

 - Proficiency in independently setting up and managing cloud services that support scaling on a global level

 - Master's or Ph.D. in Computer Science, Machine Learning or similar is a plus.


WHY JOIN REDPINE?

 - Be part of a cutting-edge company shaping the future of data in AI.

 - Work in a global-first, AI-native environment with industry-leading professionals.

 - Enjoy a competitive base salary with significant equity upside.

 - Engage with top-tier partners across AI, finance, publishing, healthcare, and more.


ABOUT REDPINE

Redpine https://redpine.ai is an AI-native, global-first company building the knowledge layer for AI. We unlock compliant access to high-quality, non-public, multi-modal data in fields like medicine, science, law, and finance, enabling data owners to monetize their content in a controlled way.

We embrace a culture of curiosity, speed, innovation and excellence. We believe in continuous learning, experimentation, and pushing the boundaries of what's possible in AI technology. You'll be working closely with the founding team, including Anders (ex-partner at VC, McKinsey), and David (ex-tech and product leader at unicorn companies Spotify, Zettle and Lunar).

We prioritize an office-first culture (in Stockholm) to fuel collaboration and connection, but we’re big on autonomy - for those days you need a "deep work" day at home or have life admin to handle.

If you are eager to make a meaningful impact in the AI space, we’d love to hear from you. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive team: if you're excited about this role but your experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway.

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer, Cloud Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
The Role

As an AI Engineer in Cloud Infrastructure at Redpine, you will design and own the foundation for a new type of AI platform, one where data access, compliance, and real-time retrieval are first-class concerns.

 

This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden and requires a valid Swedish work permit or eligibility.

 

In this role, you will:

 - Design and build the infrastructure powering our data platform and APIs

 - Work on systems that serve high-quality data to AI models and agents in real time1 https://docs.google.com/document/d/1gMpq5v2cokEJd-RUoACAZkEccMJa9BpNeUUMUgkWIxg/edit

 - Develop abstractions that make complex data access simple for developers

 - Build for a new category of workloads, where retrieval, latency, and data quality matter as much as compute

 - Make architectural decisions that will define how the platform scales globally

 - Take ownership of reliability and performance as the system evolves

The Team

We’re a small, focused and highly technical team based in Stockholm, working across infrastructure, data, and AI. The same people designing systems are building, shipping, and running them. Feedback loops are short and ownership is direct. We’re early enough that many of the core systems are still being built. You won’t be scaling someone else’s architecture, instead you’ll be defining it.

 

What We’re Looking For

 - Production experience building data infrastructure: vector database, object storage at scale, streaming/ingestion, data transfer infrastructure & pipelines

 - You have a strong understanding of cloud platforms and modern infra tooling

 - Deep experience with Kubernetes or similar orchestration systems1 https://docs.google.com/document/d/1gMpq5v2cokEJd-RUoACAZkEccMJa9BpNeUUMUgkWIxg/edit

 - A strong interest in working on systems where data, not just compute, is the core constraint

 - The judgment and ability to operate in an environment where direction evolves quickly and many problems are not yet well-defined

 - Experience in monitoring, observability, and security is a plus.1 https://docs.google.com/document/d/1gMpq5v2cokEJd-RUoACAZkEccMJa9BpNeUUMUgkWIxg/edit



Why This Role

 

You’ll be working on questions like:

 - How should AI systems access data that isn’t public?

 - What does a real-time, API-native data layer for agents look like?

 - How do you make licensed data as easy to use as scraped data, but better?

This is closer to building Spotify in the early days of music streaming than running a traditional cloud platform. Your work will define whether we build a foundational data layer or a commodity cloud service.



Practical Details

 - This role is based in Stockholm and requires a valid Swedish work permit or similar eligibility

 - Small team 10+ people, high ownership1 https://docs.google.com/document/d/1gMpq5v2cokEJd-RUoACAZkEccMJa9BpNeUUMUgkWIxg/edit

 - Backed by strong VCs and angels from OpenAI, Spotify, and Perplexity

 - Relocation support available



About Redpine

If models were the first wave of AI, and compute the second, we're building the data layer that comes next.

Only a small fraction of the world's data is on the open internet. The rest, high-quality, domain-specific, often critical, sits behind paywalls, in databases, or with rights holders. Redpine is building the infrastructure to unlock it.

We provide AI builders and autonomous agents with access to licensed, high-quality, multimodal data through a unified platform and API. The goal is simple: make AI systems more accurate, more useful, and grounded in real-world information.

We're backed by Nordic Ninja, Node VC, and Luminar, alongside angels from OpenAI, Spotify, and Perplexity.

source_jobs.title: Senior Knowledge Graph Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Why this role exists

Agents are only as good as the knowledge they can reach. Most retrieval today is flat: chunk a document, embed it, hope the nearest vector is the right answer. That breaks the moment a question needs more than one hop, needs to know which source to trust, or needs to distinguish two entities that happen to share a name. Redpine is building the layer that fixes this: a knowledge graph over the licensed data we hold across medicine, science, law, and finance, served directly to agents over our API, MCP, and CLI.

You will own that graph end to end, from raw multimodal sources to the structured, provenance-bearing knowledge that agents query in production. This is an early, high-ownership role. The decisions you make about how we model and link knowledge will shape what every agent built on Redpine can actually reason about.



Why knowledge graphs are core to agentic retrieval

Agents don't just look things up, they traverse. They follow a claim to its source, a company to its filings, a drug to its trials to the patients those trials enrolled. That kind of multi-hop reasoning needs explicit structure: typed entities, named relations, and a graph a planner can walk.



What you'll work on

 - Ontology and schema design. Define the entity types, relations, and constraints that licensed data from each domain maps into, building on the established ontologies those fields already use rather than reinventing them. You'll decide where a shared ontology helps and where a domain needs its own.

 - Entity resolution at scale. Deduplicate, canonicalize, and disambiguate entities across heterogeneous, multimodal sources, so the same thing in the world is one node in the graph.

 - Confidence and conflict. Attach confidence to every link, and define what happens when two sources disagree. Decide what the graph asserts, what it flags, and what it surfaces to the agent.

 - Provenance as a first-class property. Preserve attribution on every node and edge, back to the source document and the license that covers it. At Redpine this is not optional, it is the product.

 - Keeping the graph live. Detect when an upstream source changes and propagate that change, so the graph reflects current knowledge instead of a stale snapshot.

 - Graph-powered retrieval. Build multi-hop traversal, hybrid graph-and-vector retrieval, and the kind of structure a reranker can actually exploit.



What we're looking for

 - Deep, direct experience with knowledge graphs: graph databases, property or RDF graphs, entity resolution, and ontology design. You've shipped one, not just read about them.

 - Strong Python and a track record of building data pipelines from scratch.

 - Real experience with retrieval and RAG, ideally graph-backed, plus the judgment to know when a graph earns its keep and when it's just overhead.

 - Judgment about messy data. You can look at multi-source, contradictory input and design a schema that survives contact with reality.

 - A bias toward small, clear systems. You question whether something needs to be built before you build it.

 - A genuinely curious mind, the kind that wants to understand a domain well enough to model it honestly.


About Redpine

If models were the first wave of AI, and compute the second, we're building the data layer that comes next.

Only a small fraction of the world's data is on the open internet. The rest, high-quality, domain-specific, often critical, sits behind paywalls, in databases, or with rights holders. Redpine is building the infrastructure to unlock it.

We provide AI builders and autonomous agents with access to licensed, high-quality, multimodal data through a unified platform and API. The goal is simple: make AI systems more accurate, more useful, and grounded in real-world information.

We're backed by Nordic Ninja, Node VC, and Luminar, alongside angels from OpenAI, Spotify, and Perplexity.

source_jobs.title: Head of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
We are looking for an experienced AI native Head of Engineering for Redpine. This is a builder's role first and foremost. You will lead, but you will also be deep in the code, shaping the architecture and shipping alongside the team. You will own the how of engineering at Redpine end to end: the team, the systems, and the pace at which we ship. You report directly to our CPTO and co-founder.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

Redpine sits in a category that moves every week. The data layer for agents is being defined right now, and the model capabilities underneath it change faster than most teams can absorb. We cannot stand still, and we cannot chase every new result either. We need an engineering leader who can tell the difference, keep a growing team focused, and make sure the right new ideas reach the codebase before our competitors ship them. The job is to make the stay calm while shipping fast




RESPONSIBILITIES AND OWNERSHIP



THE TEAM

 - Build a culture and a team where strong engineers do their best work: clear ownership, low ceremony, high trust.

 - Coach engineers, and create the conditions for them to lead.

 - Inspire new ways of working and ensure that they get adopted without degrading speed


TECHNICAL DIRECTION

 - Own the architecture across the platform: data pipelines, retrieval and reranking, the knowledge graph, and the API, MCP, and CLI that agents use.

 - Recommend the build, buy, and bet calls together to CPTO, guided by the product strategy, and keep the system simple as it grows.

 - Stay accountable for reliability, latency, and cost, the things customers actually feel.


DELIVERY AND PACE

 - Set how the team plans, prioritizes, and ships, and keep cycle time short.

 - Remove blockers before they spread, and decide quickly when a decision is reversible.

 - Hold a high quality bar without turning the process into a tax.

 - Always ask how the team can be faster but still deliver quality software.


STAYING AT THE FRONTIER

 - Track the research and the model releases that matter to Redpine, and read them closely enough to hold a real view.

 - Separate the results that change how we should build from the ones that are noise.

 - Bring the useful ones back to the team in a way that makes people faster, not anxious. Adoption before admiration.


WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - You have led engineering teams and been accountable for what they shipped. This is a hard requirement, not a nice to have.

 - You have done this at a startup that scaled, ideally one we would recognize, and you can speak to what broke as it grew and what you did about it.

 - You stay close to the frontier by choice. You read the AI model cards and the papers, and you can say which new capability changes our plan and which does not.

 - You are still technical enough to earn the team's trust. You can write and review code, and be useful at it.

 - You make hard ideas land. You can take a dense result and explain, to an engineer and to a non engineer, why it matters and what we do about it.

 - You favor small, clear systems, and you question whether a thing needs to be built before you build it.


BONUS

 - Built in a regulated or data heavy domain (health, science, legal, or finance).

 - Hands on experience with retrieval, RAG, knowledge graphs, agents, or large scale data infrastructure.

 - Took a team through a real scaling step, from seed to whatever came next.

 - A public footprint you can point to: open source, writing, talks, or research.


ABOUT REDPINE

Redpine https://redpine.ai is an AI-native, global-first company building the knowledge layer for AI. We unlock compliant access to high-quality, non-public, multi-modal data in fields like medicine, science, law, and finance, enabling data owners to monetize their content in a controlled way.

We embrace a culture of curiosity, speed, innovation and excellence. We believe in continuous learning, experimentation, and pushing the boundaries of what's possible in AI techno

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer, Retrieval & Quality
source_jobs.description:
The Role

Among other things, you'll design and own the systems that decide which licensed data reaches our customers' models, and the evaluation infrastructure that proves it's better than the alternatives.

In this role, you will:

 - Design and operate the retrieval pipeline that serves licensed data to AI agents and models in real time: hybrid search, ranking, fusion, re-ranking

 - Build the eval harness that runs continuously, gates deploys on quality, and surfaces regressions before customers feel them

 - Translate research from our data science team into production systems, then own them end to end

 - Define what "good" means for Redpine's output and build the dashboards that prove it across customers, verticals, and competitors

 - Work across the stack as quality demands it, from ingestion signals through embedding pipelines to the API response



The Team

We're a small, fast growing team based in Stockholm, working across infrastructure, data, and AI. The same people designing systems are building, shipping, and running them. Feedback loops are short and ownership is direct.

You'll work closely with our data scientists, who own research, methodology, and data quality. You'll own production retrieval and the eval infrastructure that runs on top of that.

We're early enough that many of the core systems are still being defined. That means you won't be scaling someone else's architecture.



What we're looking for

 - Production experience with retrieval systems: search, ranking, recommendation systems, or RAG at scale, not just notebooks

 - Strong opinions on evaluation, both offline and online. You've designed test sets, run A/B experiments, and argued with data scientists and product managers about whether a metric reflects reality

 - Comfortable with the full retrieval stack: embeddings, vector and lexical indexes, learning-to-rank, hybrid systems & knowledge graphs

 - Have shipped, not only researched. You know the difference between a method that works in a paper and one that works in production

 - Comfort operating where direction evolves quickly and problems aren't yet well-defined

 - We work primarily in Go, Rust and Python in a multi cloud environment. We hire for judgment, not stack.



Why this role

Most retrieval and AI engineering roles are about plugging the latest models into an existing product. This isn't.

You'll be working on questions like:

 - How do you prove that licensed data produces better agent outputs than scraped data, with rigor that holds up to a customer's evaluation team?

 - What does a retrieval system look like when freshness, provenance, and rights are first-class signals alongside relevance?

 - How do you build evals that are honest enough to fail your own deploys and convincing enough to win customers?

This role sits at the core of whether Redpine is differentiated infrastructure or a nicer API on commodity vector search.



Practical details

 - This role is based in Stockholm and requires a valid Swedish work permit or similar eligibility

 - Relocation support available

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity



About Redpine

After major advancements and investments in AI models, Compute, the next wave of progress will be around Data.

Only a small fraction of the world's data is on the open internet. The rest, high-quality, domain-specific, often critical, sits behind paywalls, in databases, or with rights holders. Redpine is building the infrastructure to unlock it.

We provide AI builders and autonomous agents with access to licensed, high-quality, multimodal data through a unified platform and API. The goal is simple: make AI systems more accurate, more useful, and grounded in real-world information.

Redpine was founded by entrepreneurs and operators from companies like Spotify, McKinsey, Sana and VC. We're backed by Nordic Ninja, Node VC, and Luminar, alongside angels from OpenAI, Spotify, and Perplexity.

source_jobs.title: Growth & Operations Internship
source_jobs.description:
We are looking for an intern reporting to the Chief of Staff that wants to build operating muscles, working closely with both strategy and execution. This is not an internship where you sit on the sidelines. At Redpine, you will work directly with our Chief of Staff and CEO to keep the company running efficiently while tackling strategic initiatives that shape our trajectory. You will see how founders think, make decisions that impact our operations and culture, and own projects from conception to launch. If you are driven, adaptable, and ready to learn at hyper speed while making real impact, this is your platform.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Operational Excellence: Build systems and processes that keep us moving fast - from meeting coordination to project tracking and cross-team alignment. AI first.

 - Marketing & Events: Coordinate product launches, events, and campaigns alongside the broader team, manage social presence and campaign execution.

 - Strategic Projects: Own initiatives that matter (compliance frameworks, market research, go-to-market planning) and see them through from concept to execution.

 - Ad-hoc Priorities: Support commercial initiatives, investor relations, and leadership initiatives as they emerge - the role evolves with company needs.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - High performer: Current student or recent graduate in Business, Engineering, Economics, or similar field with a strong track record in academics or extracurriculars.

 - Operator mindset: You thrive in ambiguity, own problems end-to-end, and have a bias for action. You're not waiting for perfect information to move.

 - Clear communicator: You write and speak with precision, craft compelling narratives, and can translate strategy into action.

 - Judgment and intellectual curiosity: You ask smart questions, understand trade-offs, and have genuine interest in how startups work and how AI is reshaping industries.

 - Experience is a plus: Previous experience in startups, VC, consulting, or operational roles - or deep knowledge of finance, health, or tech - is a strong advantage.


PRACTICALITIES

 - 3 months, start date to be defined with candidates.

 - Mainly onsite, with some flexibility.

 - This is a paid internship.


WHY JOIN REDPINE?

 - Be part of a cutting-edge company shaping the future of data in AI.

 - Work in a global-first, AI-native environment with industry-leading professionals.

 - Engage with top-tier partners across AI, finance, publishing, healthcare, and more.


ABOUT REDPINE

Redpine https://redpine.ai/ is an AI-native, global-first company building the knowledge layer for AI. We unlock compliant access to high-quality, non-public, multi-modal data in fields like medicine, science, law, and finance, enabling data owners to monetize their content in a controlled way.

We embrace a culture of curiosity, speed, innovation and excellence. We believe in continuous learning, experimentation, and pushing the boundaries of what's possible in AI technology. You'll be working closely with the founding team, including Anders (ex-partner at VC, McKinsey), and David (ex-tech and product leader at unicorn companies Spotify, Zettle and Lunar).

We prioritize an office-first culture (in Stockholm) to fuel collaboration and connection, but we’re big on autonomy - for those days you need a "deep work" day at home or have life admin to handle.

If you are eager to make a meaningful impact in the AI space, we’d love to hear from you. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive team: if you're excited about this role but your experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Internship - Fall
source_jobs.description:
We are looking for a business development intern that wants to supercharge the start of their career at the intersection of AI and global business. This is not an internship where you sit on the sidelines; at Redpine, you will work directly with our founding team (ex-McKinsey, VC, Spotify) to build the partnerships that power the next generation of AI models. You will be in the engine room of a high-growth startup, helping us map global markets, identify high-value data owners, and operationalize our commercial pipeline. If you are hungry, ambitious, and ready to turn your drive into tangible impact from day one, this is your launchpad.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Partner Sourcing & Outreach: Proactively identify key decision-makers and find creative ways to open doors and secure meetings with potential data partners.

 - Deal Execution: Work alongside the founders to drive momentum in the partnership pipeline, crafting/revising pitch decks and strategic narratives that convert interest into action.

 - Market Intelligence: Research and map global data landscapes to uncover high-value opportunities and hidden gems in sectors like science, media, finance, and more.

 - Ad-hoc Projects: Support the wider team with strategic initiatives and administrative tasks to remove friction and accelerate deal flow.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Strong track record: Current student or recent graduate in Business, Engineering, or similar field, who has consistently performed at a high level in academics or extracurriculars.

 - Entrepreneurial mindset: You are a self-starter who thrives in ambiguity and is willing to "roll up your sleeves" to get the job done, no matter the task. Strong bias for action.

 - Strong communicator: You have excellent written and verbal skills, with the ability to craft compelling messages for senior stakeholders.

 - Agile fast learner: You grasp technical and commercial concepts quickly, have a genuine interest in AI, and thrive in a fast-paced environment.

 - Experience is a plus: Previous work in high-growth startups, consulting, or VC - or knowledge of sectors like media, finance, or healthcare - is a strong advantage.




WHY JOIN REDPINE?

 - Be part of a cutting-edge company shaping the future of data in AI.

 - Work in a global-first, AI-native environment with industry-leading professionals.

 - Engage with top-tier partners across AI, finance, publishing, healthcare, and more.


ABOUT REDPINE

Redpine https://redpine.ai/ is an AI-native, global-first company building the knowledge layer for AI. We unlock compliant access to high-quality, non-public, multi-modal data in fields like medicine, science, law, and finance, enabling data owners to monetize their content in a controlled way.

We embrace a culture of curiosity, speed, innovation and excellence. We believe in continuous learning, experimentation, and pushing the boundaries of what's possible in AI technology. You'll be working closely with the founding team, including Anders (ex-partner at VC, McKinsey), and David (ex-tech and product leader at unicorn companies Spotify, Zettle and Lunar).

We prioritize an office-first culture (in Stockholm) to fuel collaboration and connection, but we’re big on autonomy - for those days you need a "deep work" day at home or have life admin to handle.

If you are eager to make a meaningful impact in the AI space, we’d love to hear from you. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive team: if you're excited about this role but your experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway.

notes: Highly technical roles, AI-focused, rapid hiring pace
